---
subtitle: "Scoring Chemical, Carbon, and Maturity Risk Before Deployment"
abstract: |
  This chapter closes Part II with the feasibility layer, extending the EU Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD) framework into a scoring matrix, illustrated with two case studies.
keywords:
  - SSbD
  - chemical safety
  - carbon footprint
  - TRL risk
  - de-risking
---

# Feasibility & SSbD De-risking Matrix {#sec-ssbd-derisking-matrix}

A stakeholder journey that looks clean on paper can still fail on feasibility grounds -- a chemical substitute that is not yet safe at scale, or an infrastructure choice with a hidden carbon cost. This chapter extends the EU's Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD) framework, originally scoped to EU internal value chains, into a three-axis de-risking matrix illustrated with two worked cases.

## The Safe and Sustainable by Design Paradigm
- Extending EU Green Digital Transformation guidance to global value chains, beyond its original EU-internal scope.

## Case Study 1: Technologies
- Patent mapping to identify non-toxic chemical alternatives (e.g., PFAS replacement in semiconductor manufacturing).
- Note: solvents such as Isopropyl Alcohol (IPA) raise a distinct set of concerns (flammability, VOC emissions) from persistent PFAS compounds; treat as a separate sub-case rather than a like-for-like PFAS alternative.

## Case Study 2: Infrastructure
- De-risking next-generation data-center cooling procurement using scientometric foresight.

## The De-risking Matrix
- Three-axis scoring: chemical toxicity x carbon footprint x TRL risk.
- Output format designed to feed directly into the DPP schema (@sec-digital-product-passports).

::: {.callout-tip title="Bridge to Part III"}
Part III operationalizes everything above into standards, data-space governance, and trade-policy alignment.
:::
